## Short Answer Questions

1. In the refining of silver, the recovery of silver from silver nitrate solution involved displacement by copper metal. Write down the reaction involved.
2. Translate the following statements into chemical equations and then balance them.
(a) Hydrogen gas combines with nitrogen to form ammonia.
(b) Hydrogen sulphide gas burns in air to give water and sulphur dioxide
(c) Barium chloride reacts with aluminum sulphate to give aluminum chloride and a precipitate of barium sulphate.
(d) Potassium metal reacts with water to give potassium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
3. What is rancidity ?
4. Balance the following chemical reactions.
(a) $\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{NO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{~N}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{P}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{10}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4}$
(c) $\mathrm{HIO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{As}+\mathrm{Cl}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AsCl}_{5}$
5. Balance the chemical equations for the following important industrial processes :
(a) Molecular nitrogen and hydrogen combine to give ammonia.
(b) Molecular hydrogen and carbon monoxide react to form methanol $\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{OH}\right)$.
(c) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{C} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{CaC}_{2}$
(d) $\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{4}+\mathrm{O}_{2}+\mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
6. Represent each of the following word reaction with a balanced chemical equation.
(a) Disilane gas $\left(\mathrm{Si}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{6}\right)$ undergoes combustion to form solid silicon dioxide and water.
(b) Solid aluminium hydride is formed by a combination reaction of its two elements.
(c) When solid calcium bisulfite is heated, it decomposes to solid calcium oxide, sulfur dioxide gas, and water.
7. A large piece of zinc metal is placed in a copper (II) sulfate, $\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}$, solution. The blue solution become colourless as copper metal falls to the bottom of the container. The resulting solution contains zinc sulfate, $\mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}$. Write balanced chemical equation for the reaction.
8. A solution of $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$ is mixed with a solution of $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$. Write the molecular, total ionic and net ionic equations illustrating the reaction.
9. Write decomposition reactions for the following compounds.
(a) $\quad \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_{3}\right)_{2}$
(b) $\quad \mathrm{Ag}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$
10. Show that oxidation and reduction reactions go side by side by taking a suitable example.
11. Explain the following in terms of gain or loss of oxygen with two examples each.
(a) Oxidation
(b) Reduction
12. Can oxidation or reduction take place alone? Why or why not? What are such reactions called?
13. (a) Solution of a substance ' X ' is used for testing carbon dioxide. Write the equation of the reaction of ' X ' with carbon dioxide.
(b) How is ' X ' obtained? Write chemical equation.
14. When hydrogen gas is passed over heated copper (II) oxide, copper and steam are formed. Write the balanced chemical equation with physical states for this reaction. State what kind of chemical reaction is this?
15. What is observed when carbon dioxide gas is passed through lime water.
(a) For a short duration?
(b) For a long duration? Also write the chemical equations for the reaction involved.
16. A student added few pieces of aluminium metal to two test tubes A and B containing aqueous solutions of iron sulphate and copper sulphate. In the second part of her experiment, she added iron metal to another test tubes C and D containing aqueous solution of aluminium sulphate and copper sulphate.

In which test tube or test tube will she observe colour change? On the basis of this experiment, state which one is the most reactive metal and why?
17. Identify the type of reactions taking place in each of the following cases and write the balanced chemical equation for the reactions.
(a) Zinc reacts with silver nitrate to produce zinc nitrate and silver.
(b) Potassium iodide reacts with lead nitrate to produce potassium nitrate and lead iodide.
18. 2 g of silver chloride is taken in a china dish and the china dish is placed in sunlight for sometime. What will be your observation in this case? Write the chemical reaction involved in the form of a balanced chemical equation. Identify the type of chemical reaction.
19. Mention with reason the colour changes observed when :
(i) silver chloride is exposed to sunlight.
(ii) copper powder is strongly heated in the presence of oxygen.
(iii) a piece of zinc is dropped in copper sulphate solution.

## Long Answer Questions

1. What are different types of combination reactions ?
2. A solution of $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}$ is mixed with a solution of $\mathrm{CaCl}_{2}$. Predict what happens.
3. Balance the following equations
(a) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+\mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(d) $\mathrm{Al}+\mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AlPO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$
(e) $\quad \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}+\mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
4. Write balanced equations by predicting the products of the following reactions. Include the physical state of each element or compound.
(a) The neutralization of aqueous rubidium hydroxide with hydrobromic acid.
(b) A single-displacement reaction of calcium metal with a nitric acid solution.
(c) The combustion of solid naphthalene $\left(\mathrm{C}_{10} \mathrm{H}_{8}\right)$.
(d) A precipitation reaction involving aqueous ammonium chromate and aqueous barium bromide.
(e) The decomposition of solid aluminum hydroxide into solid aluminum oxide and gaseous water.
5. Identify the type of chemical reaction in the following statements and define each of them :
(a) Digestion of food in our body
(b) Rusting of iron
(c) Heating of manganese dioxide with aluminium powder
(d) Blue colour of copper sulphate solution disappears when iron fillings are added to it
(e) Dilute hydrochloric acid is added to sodium hydroxide solution to form sodium chloride and water.

## Reasoning Based Questions

1. Why is photosynthesis considered as an endothermic reaction?
2. Why is the amount of gas collected in one of the test tubes in electrolysis of water double of the amount collected in the other? Name this gas.
3. The oxidation of iron to rust is a problem, but the oxidation of aluminium to aluminium oxide is not why?
4. Why the green colour of ferrous sulphate salt changes to brown and produces smell of burning sulphur on strong heating?
5. Why colourless lead nitrate produces a yellow residue and brown fumes on heating?
6. When an aqueous solution of $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$ is added to common salt solution, then a white precipitate is formed. Why?
7. Why the formation of black precipitate takes place, when hydrogen sulphide gas is passed through an aqueous solution of $\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}$ ?
8. Why sodium metal reacts violently with water?
9. Why antioxidants are added to fat and oil containing food?
10. Why magnesium ribbon burns in air with a dazzling flame and forms white ash?

## HOTS Questions

1. A substance X , which is an oxide of a group 2 element, is used intensively in the cement industry. This element is present in bones also. On treatment with water it forms a solution which turns red litmus blue. Identify $X$ and also write the chemical reactions involved.
2. A solution of KBr is mixed with a solution of $\mathrm{Sr}\left(\mathrm{ClO}_{4}\right)_{2}$. Predict what happens.
3. Identify the reducing agent in the following reactions.
(a) $4 \mathrm{NH}_{3}+5 \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{NO}+6 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{F}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HF}+\mathrm{HOF}$
(c) $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+3 \mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Fe}+3 \mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(d) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
4. Write balanced equations for the reactions between each of the following oxides and water :
(a) $\mathrm{Li}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) CaO
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$
5. Represent the following chemical reaction as a balanced equation. Liquid "rubbing alcohol," $\left(\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}\right)$ reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide gas and water.
6. (a) Would you expect aluminum oxide to be a solid, liquid, or gas at room temperature?
(b) Write the balanced chemical equation for the reaction of aluminum oxide with nitric acid.
7. Consider the following two possible reactions. If a reaction does occur, write the balanced equations illustrating the reactions.
(a) A strip of tin metal is placed in an aqueous $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$ solution.
(b) A strip of silver metal is placed in an aqueous perchloric acid solution.
8. Write combination reactions that occur when the metal barium reacts with the following nonmetals.
(a) Hydrogen
(b) Sulfur
(c) Bromine
(d) Nitrogen

## Case Study Based MCQs

1. Read the following and answer any four questions from (i) to (v).

Chemical reactions involve the breaking and making of bonds between atoms to produce new substances. during a chemical reaction atoms of one element do not change into those of another element. Nor do atoms disappear from the mixture or appear from elsewhere. There are certain types of reactions. Reactions in which a single product is formed from two or more reactants is known as a combination reaction. Decomposition reactions are the reactions in which a compound breaks down into simpler compounds. Displacement and double displacement reactions are the one in which an atom or group of atom is replaced by another.Another type of reaction is redox reactions in which simultaneous oxidation and reduction takes place.
(i) Which of the following reactions involved the combination of two element?
(a) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{2}$
(b) $4 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{SO}_{3}$
(d) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}+\mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$
(ii) Consider the reaction
$\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Al} \rightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Fe}$
The above reaction is an example of
(a) combination reaction
(b) double displacement reaction
(c) decomposition reaction
(d) simple displacement reaction
(iii) The equation
$\mathrm{Mg}(s)+\mathrm{CuO}(s) \square \rightarrow \mathrm{MgO}(s)+\mathrm{Cu}(s)$ represents
(i) decomposition reaction
(ii) displacement reaction
(iii) combination reaction
(iv) double displacement reaction
(v) redox reaction
(a) (i) and (ii)
(b) (iii) and (iv)
(iv) and (v)
(c) (ii) and (v)
(d)
(iv) Which of the following is a decomposition reaction?
(a) $2 \mathrm{HgO} \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} 2 \mathrm{Hg}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} \mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow{\text { electrolysis }} \mathrm{H}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(d) All of these
(v) $\mathrm{CuO}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \square \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{Cu}$ reaction is an example of -
(a) redox reaction
(b) synthesis reaction
(c) neutralisation
(d) analysis reaction

## Exercise 21 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar)

## Text - Book Questions

1. Why should a magnesium ribbon be cleaned before burning in air?
2. Write the balanced equation for the following chemical reactions.
(i) Hydrogen + Chlorine ⟶ Hydrogen chloride
(ii) Barium chloride + Aluminium sulphate ⟶ Barium sulphate + Aluminium chloride
(iii) Sodium + Water ⟶

Sodium hydroxide + Hydrogen
3. Write a balanced chemical equation with state symbols for the following reactions.
(i) Solutions of barium chloride and sodium sulphate in water react to give insoluble barium sulphate and the solution of sodium chloride.
(ii) Sodium hydroxide solution (in water) reacts with hydrochloric acid solution (in water) to produce sodium chloride solution and water.
4. A solution of a substance ' X ' is used for white washing.
(i) Name the substance ' X ' and write its formula.
(ii) Write the reaction of the substance ' X ' with water.
5. Why is the amount of gas collected in one of the test tube in activity 1.7 of your NCERT text book double of the amount collected in the other? Name this gas.
6. Why does the colour of copper sulphate solution changes when an iron nail is dipped in it?
7. Give an example of a double displacement reaction other than the one given in activity 1.10 of your NCERT text book.
8. Identify the substances that are oxidised and the substances that are reduced in the following reactions.
(i) $4 \mathrm{Na}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s})$
(ii) $\mathrm{CuO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$

## Text - Book Exercise

1. Which of the statements about the reaction below are incorrect?
$2 \mathrm{PbO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{~Pb}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(i) Lead is getting reduced.
(ii) Carbon dioxide is getting oxidised.
(iii) Carbon is getting oxidised.
(iv) Lead oxide is getting reduced.
(a) (i) and (ii)
(b) (i), (ii) and (iii)
(c) (i) and (iii)
(d) all
2. $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Al} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Fe}$

The above reaction is an example of a :
(a) combination reaction
(b) double displacement reaction
(c) decomposition reaction
(d) displacement reaction.
3. What happens when dilute hydrochloric acid is added to iron fillings? Tick the correct answer.
(a) Hydrogen gas and iron chloride are produced.
(b) Chlorine gas and iron hydroxide are produced.
(c) No reaction takes place.
(d) Iron salt and water are produced.
4. What is a balanced chemical equation? Why should chemical equations be balanced?
5. Translate the following statements into chemical equations and then balance them.
(a) Hydrogen gas combines with nitrogen to form ammonia.
(b) Hydrogen sulphide gas burns in air to give water and sulphur dioxide.
(c) Barium chloride reacts with aluminium sulphate to give aluminium chloride and a precipitate of barium sulphate.
(d) Potassium metal reacts with water to give potssium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
6. Balance the following chemical equations.
(a) $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}+\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}+\mathrm{NaCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}+\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}$.
(d) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+\mathrm{HCl}$
7. Write the balanced chemical equations for the following reactions.
(a) Calcium hydroxide + Carbon dioxide ⟶

Calcium carbonate + water
(b) Zinc + Silver nitrate ⟶ Zinc nitrate + Silver
(c) Aluminium + Copper chloride ⟶

Aluminium chloride + Copper
(d) Barium chloride + Potassium sulphate ⟶

Barium sulphate + Potassium chloride
8. Write the balanced chemical equation for the following and identify the type of reaction in each case.
(a) Potassium bromide(aq) + Barium iodide(aq)

⟶ Potassium iodide(aq) + Barium bromide(s)
(b) Zinc carbonate(s) ⟶

Zinc oxide(s) + Carbon dioxide(s)
(c) Hydrogen(g) + Chlorine (g) ⟶

Hydrogen chloride(g)
(d) Magnesium(s) + Hydrochloric acid(aq)

⟶ Magnesium chloride(aq) + Hydrogen(g)
9. What does one mean by exothermic and endothermic reaction? Give examples.
10. Why is respiration considered an exothermic reaction? Explain.
11. Why are decomposition reactions called the opposite of combination reactions? Write equations for these reactions.
12. What is the difference between displacement and double displacement reactions? Write equations for these reactions.
13. In the refining of silver, the recovery of silver from silver nitrate solution involved displacement by copper metal. Write down the reaction involved.
14. What do you mean by a precipitation reaction? Explain by giving examples.
15. A shiny brown coloured element ' $X$ ' on heating in air becomes black in colour. Name the element ' X ' and the black coloured compound formed.
16. Why do we apply paint on iron articles?
17. Oil and fat containing food items are flushed with nitrogen. Why?
18. Explain the following terms with one example each.
(a) Corrosion
(b) Rancidity
19. Write chemical equation for decomposition reactions where energy is supplied in the form of (i) heat (ii) light (iii) electricity.

## Exemplar Questions

1. Complete the missing components/variables given as $x$ and $y$ in the following reactions
(i) $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{KI}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbI}_{2}(x)+\mathrm{KNO}_{3}(y)$
(ii) $\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+x(s)$
(iii) $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}(x)+\mathrm{H}_{2}(y)$
(iv) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{x} \mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
2. Grapes hanging on the plant do not ferment but after being plucked from the plant can be fermented. Under what conditions do these grapes ferment? Is it a chemical or a physical change?
3. During the reaction of some metals with dilute hydrochloric acid, following observations were made.
(i) Silver metal does not show any change
(ii) The temperature of the reaction mixture rises when aluminium (Al) is added.
(iii) The reaction of sodium metal is found to be highly explosive.
(iv) Some bubbles of a gas are seen when lead ( Pb ) is reacted with the acid.
Explain these observations giving suitable reasons.
4. Ferrous sulphate decomposes with the evolution of a gas having a characteristic odour of burning sulphur. Write the chemical reaction involved and identify the type of reaction.
5. You are provided with two containers made up of copper and aluminium. You are also provided with solutions of dilute HCl , dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$. In which of the above containers these solutions can be kept?

## Exercise 3

## Multiple Choice Questions

DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has four choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which ONLY ONE is correct.

1. Which of the following reactions involves the combination of two elements?
(a) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$
(b) $4 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{SO}_{3}$
(d) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}+\mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}$
2. When hydrogen sulphide gas is passed through a blue solution of copper sulphate, a black precipitate of copper sulphide is obtained and the sulphuric acid so formed remains in the solution. The reaction is an example of -
(a) a combination reaction
(b) a displacement reaction
(c) a decomposition reaction
(d) a double decomposition reaction
3. What happens when copper rod is dipped in iron sulphate solution?
(a) Copper displaces iron
(b) Blue colour of copper sulphate solution is obtained
(c) No reaction takes place
(d) Reaction is exothermic
4. A student added dilute HCl to a test tube containing zinc granules and made following observations which one is correct?
(a) The zinc surface became dull and black.
(b) A gas evolved which burns with a pop sound.
(c) The solution remained colourless.
(d) The solution becomes green in colour.
5. A dilute solution of sodium carbonate was added to two test tubes (A) containing dil HCl and (B) containing dilute NaOH . The correct observation was -
(a) a brown coloured gas liberated in test tube A.
(b) a brown coloured gas liberated in test tube B.
(c) a colourless gas liberated in test tube A.
(d) a colourless gas liberated in test tube B.
6. A balanced chemical equation is in accordance with -
(a) Avogadro's law
(b) law of multiple proportion
(c) law of conservation of mass
(d) law of gaseous volumes.
7. The equation

$$
\mathrm{Cu}+x \mathrm{HNO}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+y \mathrm{NO}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}
$$

The values of $x$ and $y$ are -
(a) 3 and 5
(b) 8 and 6
(c) 4 and 2
(d) 7 and 1
8. $\mathrm{Zn}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}($ dil $) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \uparrow$ Above reaction is -
(a) decomposition reaction
(b) single displacement reaction
(c) combination reaction
(d) synthesis reaction
9. The reaction in which two compounds exchange their ions to form two new compounds is -
(a) a displacement reaction
(b) a decomposition reaction
(c) an isomerization reaction
(d) a double displacement reaction
10. When the gases sulphur dioxide and hydrogen sulphide mix in the presence of water, the reaction is $\mathrm{SO}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+3 \mathrm{~S}$. Here hydrogen sulphide is acting as -
(a) an oxidising agent
(b) a reducing agent
(c) a dehydrating agent
(d) a catalyst
11. $\mathrm{CuO}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{Cu}$, reaction is an example of -
(a) redox reaction
(b) synthesis reaction
(c) neutralisation
(d) analysis reaction
12. A substance which oxidises itself and reduces other is known as -
(a) oxidising agent
(b) reducing agent
(c) both of these
(d) none of these
13. A redox reaction is one in which -
(a) both the substances are reduced.
(b) both the substances are oxidised.
(c) an acid is neutralised by the base.
(d) one substance is oxidised while the other is reduced.
14. In the following equations:
$\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}+x \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$, the value of $x$ is-
(a) 1
(b) 2
(c) 3
(d) 4
15. In the equation, $\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{HNO}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{NaNO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ nitric acid is acting as -
(a) an oxidising agent
(b) an acid
(c) a nitrating agent
(d) a dehydrating agent
16. $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Al} \rightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Fe}$

The above reaction is an example of $\mathrm{a}-$
(a) combination reaction
(b) double displacement reaction
(c) decomposition reaction
(d) displacement reaction
17. White silver chloride in sunlight turns to -
(a) grey
(b) yellow
(c) remain white
(d) red
18. Black and white photography uses -
(a) decomposition of silver chloride.
(b) decomposition of silver bromide.
(c) both
(d) none of these
19. When copper powder is heated it gets coated with -
(a) black copper oxide
(b) yellow copper oxide
(c) red copper oxide
(d) None of these
20. Combination of phosphorus and oxygen is an example of-
(a) oxidation
(b) reduction
(c) rancidity
(d) None of these
21. Rusting of iron is an example of -
(a) reduction
(b) redox
(c) oxidation
(d) dissociation
22. Which of the following does not corrode when exposed to the atmosphere?
(a) Iron
(b) Copper
(c) Gold
(d) Silver
23. Take about $1.0 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$ in a test tube. Heat it over a flame, a colourless gas comes out. The reaction is called a
(a) decomposition reaction
(b) displacement reaction
(c) double decomposition reaction
(d) double displacement reaction
24. Hydrogen sulphide $\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}\right)$ is a strong reducing agent. Which of the following reactions shows its reducing action?
(a) $\mathrm{Cd}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CdS}+2 \mathrm{HNO}_{3}$
(b) $\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CuS}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{FeCl}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{FeCl}_{2}+2 \mathrm{HCl}+\mathrm{S}$
(d) $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbS}+2 \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}$
25. $2 \mathrm{CuI} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{CuI}_{2}$, the reaction is -
(a) redox
(b) neutralisation
(c) oxidation
(d) reduction
26. When copper turnings are added to silver nitrate solution, a blue coloured solution is formed after some time. It is because, copper -
(a) displaces silver from the solution
(b) forms a blue coloured complex with $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$
(c) is oxidised to $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$
(d) is reduced to $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$
27. $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})$. This is -
(a) oxidation
(b) reduction
(c) redox reaction
(d) none of these

[NTSE]
(a) HCl and NaOH
(b) HCl and $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}$
(c) Na and HCl
(d) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

[JSTSE]
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ (gaseous)
(b) $\mathrm{CO}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{CH}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$

[KVPY]
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$
(b) $\mathrm{CS}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
(d) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{3}$

[JSTSE]
(a) Addition of conc. HCl to water
(b) $\mathrm{CH}_{4}(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$

33. The oxidation states of P atom in $\mathrm{POCl}_{3}, \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{PO}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{P}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{6}$, respectively are
[KVPY]
(a) $+5,+4,+4$
(b) $\quad+5,+5,+4$
(c) $+4,+4,+5$
(d) $+3,+4,+5$
34. The process of respiration is :
[JSTSE]
(a) Oxidation reaction which is endothermic
(b) Reduction reaction which is endothermic
(c) Combination reaction which is exothermic
(d) Oxidation reaction which is exothermic

[JSTSE]
(a) Silver oxide
(b) Silver nitrate
(c) Silver chloride
(d) Silve sulphide

(b) 21.00 g

(d) 2.008 g

## More than One Correct Option

DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has four choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which ONE OR MORE may be correct.

1. Which of the following statements is correct?
(a) A chemical equation tells us about the substances involved in a reaction.
(b) A chemical equation informs us about the symbols and formula of substances involved in a reaction.
(c) A chemical equation tells us about the atom or molecules of the reactants and products involved in a reaction.
(d) A chemical equation represents energy changes during a reaction.
2. In the reaction $\mathrm{PbO}+\mathrm{C} \rightarrow \mathrm{Pb}+\mathrm{CO}$
(a) PbO is reduced
(b) C acts as a oxidising agent
(c) C acts as a reducing agent
(d) this reaction does not represent redox reaction
3. Lead nitrate on decomposition gives-
(a) lead oxide
(b) nitrogen dioxide
(c) oxygen
(d) nitrogen
4. Heating of limestone produces -
(a) quick lime
(b) carbon dioxide
(c) carbon monoxide
(d) slaked lime
5. Ferrous sulphate on heating produces -
(a) ferric oxide
(b) sulphur dioxide
(c) oxygen
(d) water
6. Rancidity can be prevented by -
(a) adding anti-oxidants
(b) packaging oily food in nitrogen gas
(c) packaging oily food in argon gas
(d) use of spices
7. Which of the following are endothermic process?
(a) boiling of water
(b) disolving salts in water
(c) dissolving conc. $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$ in water
(d) evaporation of water
8. Which of the following is (are) an example of a displacement reaction?
(a) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$
(b) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{Cu}+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CuNO}_{3}+2 \mathrm{Ag}$
(d) $\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CuS}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
9. The process of oxidation involves -
(a) the addition of oxygen atoms.
(b) the acceptance of hydrogen atoms.
(c) the release of hydrogen atoms.
(d) neither acceptance nor release of hydrogen atoms.
10. Following reaction describes the rusting of iron $4 \mathrm{Fe}+3 \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$
Which one of the following statement is (are) correct?
(a) This is an example of a oxidation reaction.
(b) Metallic iron is oxidised to $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$.
(c) Fe is reduced to $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$.
(d) Metallic iron in this is an example of reduction reaction.
11. Potassium chlorate on decomposition gives-
(a) potassium chloride
(b) oxygen gas
(c) chlorine gas
(d) potassium oxide
12. $2 \mathrm{Mg}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{MgO}$, the above reaction is an example of-
(a) decomposition reaction
(b) displacement reaction
(c) combination reaction
(d) oxidation reaction
13. Ammonia gas is formed by the combination of nitrogen and hydrogen

$$
\mathrm{N}_{2}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}
$$

In the above equation
(a) Nitrogen and hydrogen are reactants.
(b) One molecule of nitrogen combines with 3 molecules of hydrogen and form two molecules of ammonia.
(c) One volume of nitrogen and three volume of hydrogen combine and give 2 volumes of ammonia gas.
(d) Reactants and products are not gaseous.
14. Double decomposition reactions are-
(a) $\mathrm{Mg}+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{MgSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Cu}$
(b) $\mathrm{KOH}+\mathrm{HNO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{KNO}_{3}$
(c) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{~S}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$
(d) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{HCl}$
15. $2 \mathrm{SO}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{SO}_{3}+42 \mathrm{kcal}$. The above reaction is-
(a) endothermic reaction
(b) exothermic reaction
(c) combination reaction
(d) displacement reaction

## Passage Based Questions

DIRECTIONS : Study the given paragraph(s) and answer the following questions.

## Passage

An element placed above in the activity series will replace the element placed below it from its aqueous solution. Reactivity of metals towards other elements decreases as you go down the series. The stability of their compounds also decreases.
Any metal above hydrogen reacts with acids, replaces hydrogen from acids.
The more active a metal, the more strongly it holds on to oxygen in an oxide and therefore, the more strongly the oxide resists decomposition into its elements upon heating.

1. Copper displaces which of the following metals from its salt solution?
(a) Zinc
(b) Iron
(c) Silver
(d) Nickel
2. Which of the following oxides can be decomposed with difficulty?
(a) CuO
(b) ZnO
(c) AgO
(d) CrO
3. Which of the following statement is correct?
(a) Elements near the top of the series are never found free in nature.
(b) Elements near the bottom of the series are often found free in nature.
(c) Elements near the top of the series are found free in nature.
(d) both (a) and (b)

## Assertion \& Reason

DIRECTIONS : Each of these questions contains an Assertion followed by reason. Read them carefully and answer the question on the basis of following options. You have to select the one that best describes the two statements.
(a) If both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
(b) If both Assertion and Reason are correct, but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
(c) If Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
(d) If Assertion is incorrect but Reason is correct.

1. Assertion : Stannous chloride gives grey precipitate with mercuric chloride, but stannic chloride does not do so.
Reason : Stannous chloride is a powerful oxidising agent which oxidises mercuric chloride to mercury.
2. Assertion : Corrosion of iron is commonly known as rusting.
Reason : Corrosion of iron occurs in presence of water and air.
3. Assertion : In a reaction
$\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$,
Zn is a reductant but itself get oxidized.
Reason : In a redox reaction, oxidant is reduced by accepting electrons and reductant is oxidized by losing electrons.

Column I
(A) $\mathrm{KClO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\Delta}$
(B) $\mathrm{ZnCO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\Delta}$
(C) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\Delta}$
(D) $\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{6} \xrightarrow{\Delta}$

|  | A | B | C | D |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| (a) | p | s,r | q,r | q,r |
| (b) | p | q,r | s,r | r,p |
| (c) | q,r | s,p | p,s | r |
| (d) | r | q | s | p |

## Column II

(p) $\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(q) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(r) $\quad \mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(s) ZnO
(s) ZnO
4. Assertion : A reducing agent is a substance which can either accept electron.
Reason : A substance which helps in oxidation is known as reducing agent.
5. Assertion : The balancing of chemical equations is based on law of conservation of mass.
Reason : Total mass of reactants is equal to total mass of products.

(a) oxidation
(b) reduction
(c) combustion
(d) neutralization

(b) +4

(b) +6
(c) +4

(b) $2 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

(c) $x=8, y=4, z=4$
(d) $x=5, y=3, z=4$

DIRECTIONS (Qs. 24-29) : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has 4 choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which ONE OR MORE may be correct.

(c) $\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}+\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{5} \mathrm{OH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOC}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{5}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

When $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ changes to $\mathrm{Sn}^{4+}$ in a reaction then
(a) $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ loses two electrons.
(b) $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ gains two electrons.

DIRECTIONS (Qs. 30-32) : Each of these questions contains an assertion followed by reason. Read them carefully and answer the question on the basis of following options. You have to select the one that best describes the two statements.
(a) If both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
(b) If both Assertion and Reason are correct, but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
(c) If Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
(d) If Assertion is incorrect but Reason is correct.
30. Assertion : In a reaction
$\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$
Zn is a reductant but itself gets oxidized.
Reason : In a redox reaction, oxidant is reduced by accepting electrons and reductant is oxidized by losing electrons.
31. Assertion : $\mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{F}_{2}(\mathrm{~s}) \rightarrow \mathrm{MgF}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$, magnesium loses electrons and acts as a reducing agent.
Reason : Reduction in general means acceptance of electron(s) by a reactant.
32. Assertion : In a redox reaction, the oxidation number of the oxidant decreases, while that of reductant increases.
Reason : Oxidant gains electron (s) reductant loses electrons (s).

## Passage Based Questions

DIRECTIONS (Qs. 35-37) : Study the given paragraph(s) and answer the following questions.

## Passage

Oxidation number is defined as the charge which an atom of the element has in its ion or appears to have when present in the combined state with other atoms.

There are certain rules for assigning oxidation number. Some of these rules are given below.

- The oxidation number of all the atoms of different elements in their respective elementary states and allotropic forms is taken to be zero.
- The O.N. of a monoatomic ion is the same as the charge on it.
- The O.N. of hydrogen is +1 when combined with nonmetals and is -1 when combined with active metals called metal hydrides
- The O.N. of alkali metals (Li, Na, K, etc.) is always + 1 and that of alkaline earth metals ( $\mathrm{Be}, \mathrm{Mg}, \mathrm{Ca}$, etc.) is +2 .
- The algebraic sum of the O.N. of all the atoms in a compound must be zero. In polyatomic ion, the algebraic
sum of all the O.N.'s of atoms of the ion must equal the charge on the ion.

35. What is the oxidation number of P in $\mathrm{PO}_{4}^{3-}$ and S in $\mathrm{SO}_{4}^{2-}$ ?
(a) $\mathrm{P}=+5, \mathrm{~S}=+3$
(b) $\mathrm{P}=+3 . \mathrm{S}=+5$
(c) $\mathrm{P}=+5, \mathrm{~S}=+6$
(d) $\mathrm{P}=+6, \mathrm{~S}=+6$
36. Oxygen has an oxidation state of +2 in the compound :
(a) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $\mathrm{F}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
37. What is the correct increasing order of oxidation state of nitrogen in following compounds?
$\mathrm{NH}_{3}, \mathrm{~N}_{2}, \mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}, \mathrm{NO}_{2}$
(a) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{N}_{2}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}$
(b) $\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{NH}_{3}$
(c) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}<\mathrm{N}_{2}$
(d) $\mathrm{NH}_{3}<\mathrm{NH}_{2} \mathrm{OH}<\mathrm{N}_{2}<\mathrm{NO}_{2}$

## Integer Type Questions

DIRECTIONS (Qs. 38-42) : Following are integer based questions. Each question, when worked out will result in one integer from 0 to 9 (both inclusive).
38. Among $\mathrm{NH}_{3}, \mathrm{HNO}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{Mg}_{3} \mathrm{~N}_{2}$ how many molecules have nitrogen in negative oxidation state?
39. When $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}$ is converted into $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{CrO}_{4}$, then what is the change in oxidation number of chromium?
40. Consider the following reaction
$\mathrm{PbS}+n \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{PbSO}_{4}+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
What is the value of coefficient ' $n$ ' in the above equation?
41. Find the oxidation number of S in $\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3}\right)_{2} \mathrm{SO}$.
42. Find the difference in oxidation states of nitrogen in nitrous acid and nitric acid.

## Short Answer Questions

1. $2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{eq})+2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})$

The displacement reaction occurs because copper is more reactive than silver. Shining white deposit of silver is formed on the copper metal and the solution becomes blue due to the formation of copper nitrate.
2. (a) $3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{N}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(\mathrm{~g})$
(b) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+2 \mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

$$
2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{BaSO}_{4} \downarrow
$$

3. When food material left for longer time, the fat and oil present in them undergoes oxidation process that results in change of taste and smell of the food and make it unfit for consumption. The process of such oxidation of fat and oil is known as rancidity.
4. (a) $\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{NO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{~N}_{2} \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{P}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{10}+6 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{HIO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(d) $2 \mathrm{As}+5 \mathrm{Cl}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{AsCl}_{5}$
5. (a) $\mathrm{N}_{2}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}$
(b) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2}+\mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{OH}$
(c) $\mathrm{CaO}+3 \mathrm{C} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{CaC}_{2}$
(d) $2 \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{4}+\mathrm{O}_{2}+4 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
6. (a) $2 \mathrm{Si}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{6}(\mathrm{~g})+7 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{SiO}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})+6 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
(b) $2 \mathrm{Al}(\mathrm{s})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlH}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})$
(c) $\mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HSO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{~s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
7. In this displacement reaction, the more active metal, zinc, displaces the ions of the less active metal, copper, from the aqueous solution of copper sulphate.
The balanced chemical equation is
$\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$
8. $2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ag}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{KNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})$
$2 \mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{NO}_{3}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{~K}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{S}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$

$$
\mathrm{Ag}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{~K}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{NO}_{3}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})
$$

$2 \mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{S}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ag}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~s})$
9. (a) $\mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{~s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
(b) $2 \mathrm{Ag}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(c) $\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{NO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{NO}(\mathrm{g})$
10. $\mathrm{ZnO}+\mathrm{C} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Zn}+\mathrm{CO}$

If a substance gains oxygen during a reaction it is said to be oxidised and if a substance loses oxygen it is said to be reduced. In the above reaction carbon is oxidised to carbon monoxide, while zinc oxide is reduced to zinc. Thus, oxidation and reduction reactions are going side by side in the above reaction.
11. (a) Oxidation is a reaction which involves addition or gain of oxygen. For example,
$4 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{O} ; \quad 2 \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow{\text { Heat }} 2 \mathrm{CuO}$
(b) Reduction is a reaction which involves loss or removal of oxygen, For example,
$\mathrm{CuO}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \xrightarrow{\text { Heat }} \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} ; \quad \mathrm{ZnO}+\mathrm{C} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}+\mathrm{CO}$
12. Oxidation or reduction cannot take place alone. This is because if one substance loses oxygen, i.e., undergoes reduction, there must be another substance to take up this oxygen, i.e., undergo oxidation. Similarly, if one substance loses hydrogen, i.e., undergoes oxidation, there must be another substance to take up this hydrogen, i.e., undergo reduction. Hence, oxidation and reduction always take place together. That is why, these reactions are called redox reactions.
13. (a) Solution of a substance X can be of calcium hydroxide.
$\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \underset{\text { (White ppt.) }}{\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) ' X ' can be obtained by the reaction of calcium oxide with water.
$\mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}+$ Heat
14. $\mathrm{CuO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \xrightarrow{\text { Heat }} \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g})$

The above chemical reaction is a redox reaction.
15. (a) Lime water turns milky due to the formation of $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$ which is insoluble in water.
$\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
Lime water White ppt.
(b) A clear solution is obtained due to the formation of calcium bicarbonate $\mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_{3}\right)_{2}$ which is soluble in water.
$\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$

B $-\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}$ D $-\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}$
$3 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{Al} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}+3 \mathrm{Fe}$
(A)
$3 \mathrm{CuSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{Al} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}+\mathrm{Cu}$
(B)
$\mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}+\mathrm{Fe} \longrightarrow$ No reaction
(C)
$\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Fe} \longrightarrow \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Cu}$
(D)

Color change occurs in test tube A, B and D, no color change will be observed in test tube ( C ) becuase there is no reaction.
Al is most reactive because it displace copper and iron from their sulphate salts.
17. (a) $\mathrm{Zn}+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+2 \mathrm{Ag}$

As zinc is more reactive than silver, therefore it displaces silver from its nitrate solution.
It is a displacement reaction.
(b) $2 \mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{KNO}_{3}+\mathrm{PbI}_{2}$
(yellow)
A yellow precipitate of lead iodide is formed. It is a precipitation reaction.
18. When 2 g of AgCl taken in a china dish and placed in sunlight then it turns to black/grey metallic colured silver along with the liberation of chlorine gas.
The balanced chemical reaction will be-
$2 \mathrm{AgCl} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}+\mathrm{Cl}_{2}$
This is a decomposition reaction. As the decomposition reaction is carried out by sunlight so it is called photolytic decomposition reaction.
19. (i) Photo decomposition of silver chloride takes place which results in formation of silver and colour changing to grey.

$$
2 \mathrm{AgCl}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}+2 \mathrm{Cl}_{2}
$$

(ii) Copper undergoes oxidation and black coloured copper oxide is formed.

$$
2 \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{CuO}
$$

(iii) Zinc displaces copper from its solution and colour of the solution changes from blue to colourless.

$$
\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \text { (aq.) } \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{ZnSO}_{4} \text { (aq.) }
$$

## Long Answer Questions

1. There are three different types of combination reactions:
(a) Combination of an element with another element to form a compound.
$2 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
(b) Combination of two or more compounds to form a new compound.

$$
\mathrm{MgO}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{MgCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})
$$

(c) The combination of an element and a compound to form a new compound.

$$
2 \mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})
$$

2. The four ions involved are $\mathrm{Na}^{+}, \mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}, \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$. The combinations of $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$with $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$and $\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}$ with $\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}$ produce the compounds NaCl and $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$. If both of these compounds are soluble, no reaction occurs. In this case, however, $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$ is insoluble. Thus a reaction occurs which we can illustrate with a balanced chemical equation.
$\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{CaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq})$
The equation written in total ionic form is

$$
\begin{aligned}
2 \mathrm{Na}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq}) & +\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) \\
& \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{Na}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})
\end{aligned}
$$

The net ionic equation will be
$\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})$
3. (a) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$
(b) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$
(d) $2 \mathrm{Al}+2 \mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlPO}_{4}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}$
(e) $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCl}_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
4. (a) $\mathrm{HBr}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{RbOH}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{RbBr}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
(b) $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{HNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(c) $\mathrm{C}_{10} \mathrm{H}_{8}(\mathrm{~s})+12 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 10 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
(d) $\left(\mathrm{NH}_{4}\right)_{2} \mathrm{CrO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{BaBr}_{2}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$

$$
\mathrm{BaCrO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Br}(\mathrm{aq})
$$

(e) $2 \mathrm{Al}(\mathrm{OH})_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g})$
5. (a) Decomposition reaction : Carbohydrates are broken down to form glucose. Complex molecule broken down into simpler molecule during the digestion of food. Therefore it is a decomposition reaction.
(b) Redox reaction

Oxidation : $\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
Reduction : $\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+4 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
Overall : $2 \mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) +2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
(c) Displacement reaction
$3 \mathrm{MnO}_{2}+4 \mathrm{Al} \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{Mn}+2 \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$

Aluminium being higher in reactivity series displaces Mn from its compound. Therefore it is an example of displacement reaction.
(d) Displacement reaction
$\mathrm{Fe}+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Cu}$
Iron is placed at higher position than copper in reactivity series. Therefore iron displace copper from copper sulphate solution which result in the formation of $\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}$ leaving behind copper.
(e) Neutralisation reaction
$\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
NaOH which is a base react with hydrochloric acid leads to formation of sodium chloride (salt) and water. This is an example of neutralisation reaction.

## Reasoning Based Questions

1. Photosynthesis is an endothermic reaction because light energy in the form of sun light is absorbed during the process by green plants.
2. It is because on electrolysis water decomposes to form hydrogen and oxygen in the ratio of $2: 1$ by volume. The gas with double volume is hydrogen.
3. Iron is oxidized in presence of atmospheric oxygen and water to form iron oxide trihydrate $\left(\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3} \cdot 3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)$, known as rust. It peels off iron surface exposing fresh iron surface for further rusting. While, aluminium, oxidized in presence of atmospheric oxygen to form a thin, transparent and non water-soluble layer of aluminium oxide $\left(\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}\right)$, which shield the metal from further oxidation and maintains its metallic shine. Hence, the oxidation of iron to rust is a problem for structural engineers but the oxidation of aluminium is not.
4. Decomposition reaction
$\underset{\text { (green) }}{2 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{\text { (brown) }}{\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})}+\mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{SO}_{3}(\mathrm{~g})}$
Smell of sulphur is due to the formation of $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$ gases.
5. $\underset{\text { (colourless) }}{2 \mathrm{~Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{~s})} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{\text { (yellow) }}{2 \mathrm{PbO}(\mathrm{s})}+\underset{\text { (brown fumes) }}{4 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})}+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
6. $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})$ white ppt.
Double displacement reaction.
7. $\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CuS}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})$ Black ppt.
Double displacement reaction.
8. Reaction between sodium metal and water is highly exothermic. During the course of the reaction hydrogen gas produces which catches fire easily.
9. When antioxidants are added to food, they act as reducing agent. Therefore the fat and oil present in the food do not get oxidised easily consequently prevent rancidity.
10. When magnesium ribbon burns in air, addition of oxygen to magnesium takes place to produce magnesium oxide. This process accompanied with the evolution of heat and light due to which dazzling flame observed.

## HOTS Questions

1. Substance calcium oxide.
$\mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
$\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}$ turns red litmus blue.
2. The four ions involved are $\mathrm{K}^{+}, \mathrm{Br}^{-}, \mathrm{Sr}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{ClO}_{4}^{-}$. An exchange of ions produces the compounds $\mathrm{KClO}_{4}$ and $\mathrm{SrBr}_{2}$. Both of these compounds are soluble, so no reaction occurs.
3. (a) Ammonia $\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)$
(b) Water $\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)$ act as reducing agents
(c) Carbon monoxide (CO)
(d) Hydrogen

Hint-Reducing agents are those substances which have the ability of adding hydrogen or removing oxygen from the other substances.
4. (a) $\mathrm{Li}_{2} \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{LiOH}$
(b) $\mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}$
(c) $\mathrm{SO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
5. First, represent the name of the species involved as reactants and products with formulae and indicate their physical states.

$$
\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})
$$

Next, balance carbon. Place a coefficient of 3 in front of $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$ to balance the carbons in $\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}$. Balance the eight hydrogens in $\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}$ by adding a coefficient of 4 in front of $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$.
$\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
Now note that there are 10 oxygens on the right. On the left, one oxygen is in $\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}$ so nine are needed from $\mathrm{O}_{2}$. To get an odd number of oxygens from $\mathrm{O}_{2}$ we need to use a fractional coefficient, in this case $\frac{9}{2}$ (i.e., $\frac{9}{2} \times 2=9$ )

$$
\mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\frac{9}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})
$$

Finally, we need to clear the fraction so that all coefficients are whole numbers. Multiply the whole equation by 2 and do a quick check.

$$
2 \mathrm{C}_{3} \mathrm{H}_{8} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+9 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 6 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+8 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})
$$

6. (a) Because aluminum oxide is the oxide of a metal, we would expect it to be an ionic solid. Indeed it is, and it has a very high melting point, $2072^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.
(b) In its compounds, aluminum has a $3+$ charge, $\mathrm{Al}^{3+}$, the oxide ion is $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$, consequently, the formula of aluminum oxide is $\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$. Metal oxides tend to be basic and therefore reacts with acids to form salts and water. In this case the salt formed is aluminum nitrate, $\mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{3}$. The balanced chemical equation is
$\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+6 \mathrm{HNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
7. In the activity series that Sn is higher in the series than Ag . Therefore Sn replaces $\mathrm{Ag}+$ ions from solution.
(a) $\mathrm{Sn}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Sn}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$
(b) In the activity series, $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ is in higher position than Ag . Therefore, $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ replaces $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$, but the reverse reaction, the replacement of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$by Ag , does not occur. No reaction occurs.
8. (a) $\mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaH}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$
(b) $8 \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{S}_{8}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 8 \mathrm{BaS}(\mathrm{s})$
(c) $\mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Br}_{2}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaBr}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$
(d) $3 \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{N}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ba}_{3} \mathrm{~N}_{2}(\mathrm{~s})$

b. Copper is displaced by Mg .

## Text - Book Questions

1. Magnesium ribbon has a coating of basic magnesium carbonate on its surface, which is formed by a slow action of moist air on it. Therefore before burning it in air, it is cleaned by a sand paper which removes the protective layer of basic magnesium carbonate from the surface of magnesium ribbon, so that it can easily burn.
2. (i) $\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{g})$
(ii) $3 \mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$

$$
3 \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})
$$

(iii) $2 \mathrm{Na}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
3. (i) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$

$$
\mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq})
$$

(ii) $\mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{aq})$
4. (i) Quick lime- CaO .
(ii) $\mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$
5. The gases formed at two electrodes are produced due to decomposition of water on passing electricity. Since the electrolysis of water produces 2 volume of hydrogen gas and 1 volume of oxygen gas, thus we can say that the ratio of hydrogen and oxygen in water is $2: 1$ by volume and hence the amount of gas collected in one test tube will be double of the amount collected in other.
6. As iron is more reactive than copper thus it displaces copper from copper sulphate solution.

$$
\underset{\text { (Blue) }}{\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{~s})}+\underset{\text { (Green) }}{\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})} \longrightarrow \underset{(\mathrm{aq})}{\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})}+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{~s})
$$

In this way concentration of copper sulphate decreases while concentration of ferrous sulphate increases. As a result colour changes from blue to green.
7. $\quad \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{OH}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow$
Aluminium
Ammonium
chloride
hydroxide

$$
\underset{\substack{\text { Aluminium } \\ \text { hydroxide }}}{\mathrm{Al}(\mathrm{OH})_{3}(\mathrm{~s})}+\underset{\substack{\text { Ammonium } \\ \text { chloride }}}{3 \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{Cl}(\mathrm{aq})}
$$

8. (i)

Therefore, Na is oxidised to $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$and $\mathrm{O}_{2}$ is reduced to $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$.
(ii)

Therefore, $\stackrel{+2}{\mathrm{CuO}}$ is reduced to Cu and $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ is oxidised to $\mathrm{H}^{+}$.

## Text - Book Exercise

1. (a) The statements (i) and (ii) are incorrect because PbO is getting reduced, not Pb , similarly C is getting oxidized not $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$.
2. (d) The given reaction is an example of displacement reaction as in this reaction Al displaces Fe from $\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$.
3. (a) $\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{FeCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
4. Balanced chemical equation: - A chemical equation is said to be balanced if the number of atoms of each element participating in the reaction are equal on both
side of the equation. The chemical reaction should be balanced because law of conservation of mass states that 'In a chemical reaction total mass of the reactant must be equal to the total mass of the product'.
5. (a) $\mathrm{N}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(\mathrm{~g})$
(b) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+2 \mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(c) $3 \mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Al}_{2}\left(\mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \longrightarrow$

$$
3 \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})
$$

(d) $2 \mathrm{~K}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
6. (a) $2 \mathrm{HNO}_{3}+\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $2 \mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(c) $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}+\mathrm{NaCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}+\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}$
(d) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{HCl}$
7. (a) $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}+\mathrm{CO}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(b) $\mathrm{Zn}+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Zn}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}+2 \mathrm{Ag}$
(c) $2 \mathrm{Al}+3 \mathrm{CuCl}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}+3 \mathrm{Cu}$
(d) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}+\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{KCl}$.
8. (a) $2 \mathrm{KBr}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{BaI}_{2}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KI}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{BaBr}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})$
(b) $\mathrm{ZnCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{ZnO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(c) $\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{g})$
(d) $\mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{g})+2 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{MgCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
9. Exothermic reaction : - Those reactions which occur with the evolution of heat are exothermic reactions. e.g. $2 \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \xrightarrow[\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\text { heat }]{ }$
Endothermic reaction : - Those reactions which occur with the absorption of heat are endothermic reactions. e.g.
$2 \mathrm{~Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} 2 \mathrm{PbO}(\mathrm{s})+4 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
10. Respiration is the most important biochemical reaction which releases energy in the cell. When we breathe air (oxygen) enters into our lungs and oxygen binds itself to haemoglobin present in red blood cells (RBC). It is then carried to million of cells present in our body. Respiration occurs in these cells and is accompanied by the combustion of glucose $\left(\mathrm{C}_{6} \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_{6}\right)$ producing carbon dioxide ( $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$ ) and water ( $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$ ). A large amount of energy is released in this chemical reaction.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/3a0f04db-c54c-4692-a520-39c06e6b7649-044.jpg?height=81&width=397&top_left_y=2033&top_left_x=264)

$$
6 \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+6 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+2820 \mathrm{KJ}
$$

Since the process is accompanied by release of energy so it is considered as an exothermic reaction.
11. During decomposition reaction a single reactant decompose to form two or more products, whereas in combination reaction two or more reactants react to form a single product. e.g.
$\mathrm{N}_{2}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}$ (Combination reaction)
$2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{H}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2}$ (Decomposition reaction)
12. Displacement reaction : - In this reaction an element which is more reactive displaces the other element which is less reactive from its salt solution. e.g.
$\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})$
Double displacement reaction : - In this reaction exchange of ions between two reactants takes place. e.g.
$\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq})$
13. $\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_{3}\right)_{2}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})$
14. Those reactions which produce an insoluble product or precipitate are known as precipitation reaction. e.g. $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{NaCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s}) \downarrow+\mathrm{NaNO}_{3}(\mathrm{aq})$
15. $\underset{\text { brown }}{2 \mathrm{Cu}}+\underset{\text { from air }}{\mathrm{O}_{2}} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{\text { Cuprous Oxide }}{2 \mathrm{CuO}}$ (Black)
16. To prevent iron articles from rusting, we apply paint on it, by which iron does not come in contact with air and moisture which causes rusting.
17. When the oil and fat containing food is surrounded by unreactive gas nitrogen, oxygen become unavailable to cause oxidation and therefore prevent rancidity.
18. Corrosion : - It is a process in which metals are eaten up gradually by the action of air, moisture or a chemical on their surface. For example rusting of iron.
Rancidity - When fat and oil containing food is oxidised with air or oxygen, their smell and taste changes. This process is called rancidity.
19. (i) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{\text { heat }} \mathrm{CaO}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(ii) $2 \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s}) \xrightarrow{\text { sunlight }} 2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$
(iii) $2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l}) \xrightarrow{\text { electricity }} 2 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

## Exemplar Questions

1. (i) $x \rightarrow(\mathrm{~s})$

$$
y \rightarrow(\mathrm{aq})
$$

(ii) $x \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}$
(iii) $x \rightarrow(\mathrm{aq}), y \rightarrow(\mathrm{~g})$
(iv) $x \rightarrow$ Heat
2. Grapes when attached to the plants are living and therefore their own immune system prevents fermentation. The microbes can grow in the plucked grapes and under anaerobic conditions these can be fermented. This is a chemical change.
3. (i) Silver metal does not react with dilute HCl .
(ii) The temperature of the reaction mixture rises when aluminium is added because it is an exothermic reaction.
(iii) Reaction of sodium metal is found to be highly explosive because it is an exothermic reaction.
(iv) When lead is treated with hydrochloric acid, bubbles of hydrogen gas are evolved.
$\mathrm{Pb}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{PbCl}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2}$
4. $2 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s}) \xrightarrow{\text { Heat }} \mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{SO}_{3}(\mathrm{~g})$ It is a thermal decomposition reaction.
5. (A) When solutions are kept in copper container.
(i) Dilute HCl

Copper does not react with dilute HCl . Therefore, it can be kept.
(ii) Dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$

Nitric acid acts as a strong oxidising agent and reacts with copper vessel, therefore cannot be kept.
(iii) $\mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$

Zinc is more reactive than copper ( Cu ) therefore, no displacement reaction occurs and hence can be kept.
(iv) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

Copper does not react with water. Therefore, can be kept.
(B) When solutions are kept in aluminium containers.
(i) Dilute HCl

Aluminium reacts with dilute HCl to form its salt and hydrogen is evolved. Therefore, cannot be kept.
$2 \mathrm{Al}+6 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}$
(ii) Dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$

Aluminium gets oxidised by dilute $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}$ to form a layer of $\mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ and cannot be kept.
(iii) $\mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$

Aluminium being more reactive than zinc can displace zinc ion from the solution. Therefore, the solution cannot be kept.
$2 \mathrm{Al}+3 \mathrm{ZnCl}_{2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{AlCl}_{3}+3 \mathrm{Zn}$
(iv) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$

Aluminium does not react with cold or hot water.
Therefore, water can be kept.
Aluminium is attacked by steam to form aluminium oxide and hydrogen gas.
$2 \mathrm{~A} 1(\mathrm{~s})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$

30. (*) Let the oxidation state of S be $x$
(i) $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}$

$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore 2 & +x=0 \\
& x=-2

(ii) $\mathrm{CS}_{2}$

$$
4+2 x=0 \Rightarrow x=-2
$$

(iii) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2(+1)+x+4(-2)=0 \\
& 2+x-8=0 \\
& x=+6
\end{aligned}
$$

(iv) $\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{3}$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2(+1)+x+3(-2)=0 \\
& 2+x-6=0 \\
& x=+4
\end{aligned}
$$

## Think out of the Box

## Case Study-1

1. White fumes of ammonium chloride form by the combination of $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ and HCl . Thus, gas Y and Z are HCl and $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ respectively. Therefore, HCl gas should form by the reaction of hydrogen gas with chlorine gas ( $\mathrm{Cl}_{2}$ ). The chemical reaction involved in the process can be written as follows:
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/3a0f04db-c54c-4692-a520-39c06e6b7649-049.jpg?height=222&width=794&top_left_y=1649&top_left_x=262)
2. In the first reaction the green colour of ferrous sulphate changes to brown due to the formation of ferric oxide.
$\underset{\substack{\text { Ferrous sulphate } \\ \text { (green colour) }}}{2 \mathrm{FeSO}_{4}(\mathrm{~s})} \xrightarrow[\text { decomposition }]{\Delta} \underset{\substack{\text { Ferric oxide } \\ \text { (brown colour) }}}{\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}(\mathrm{~s})}+\underset{\substack{\text { Sulphur } \\ \text { dioxide }}}{\mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})}+\underset{\substack{\text { Sulphur } \\ \text { trioxide }}}{\mathrm{SO}_{3}(\mathrm{~g})}$

Gas X may be either $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ or $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$ and Y may be either $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ or $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$.
As given, X forms sulfurous acid, $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{3}$ that means X will be $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ and Y forms sulfuric acid, $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$ that means Y will be $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$. Thus, X and Y are $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{SO}_{3}$ respectively.

$$
\underset{(\mathrm{X})}{\mathrm{SO}_{2}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}} \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Sulfurous acid }}{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{3}}
$$

$$
\underset{(\mathrm{Y})}{\mathrm{SO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}} \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Sulfuric acid }}{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}}
$$

Molar mass of $\mathrm{X}=$ atomic mass of sulphur +2 (atomic mass of oxygen)
$=32+2(16)=64 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}$.
Similarly,
Molar mass of $\mathrm{Y}=32+3(16)=80 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}$.
3. As the given properties, it is suggested that the metal M is Na and gas is $\mathrm{Cl}_{2}$. Therefore, salt is NaCl , which exhibits white colour. NaCl on electrical decomposition produces Na and $\mathrm{Cl}_{2}$. So, this reaction is a type of decomposition reaction.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/3a0f04db-c54c-4692-a520-39c06e6b7649-049.jpg?height=159&width=615&top_left_y=1288&top_left_x=1160)
